name: Prague
tagline: Fairy-Tale Architecture and Central European Charm at a Discount
description: Prague is one of Europe's most beautiful cities, with medieval architecture, a world-class beer culture, and a cost of living well below Western Europe. The city has excellent public transport, fast internet, and a growing international community. While it lacks the beaches of Southern Europe, Prague compensates with stunning seasons, incredible cultural offerings, and a central location that makes all of Europe accessible by train or budget flight.

quickFacts.visa: 90-day Schengen visa-free for US citizens. Long-term visa available for financially independent persons (proof of funds required). Zivnostenský list (trade license) also used for residency. Path to permanent residency after 5 years.
quickFacts.language: Czech, English widely spoken in Prague
quickFacts.safetyRating: High – one of Europe's safest capitals
quickFacts.climate: Continental with warm summers and cold, snowy winters

housing.description: 1BR apartment in Vinohrady or Žižkov, popular expat neighborhoods

dining.description: Traditional Czech pubs and restaurants 3-4x/week; beer is cheaper than water

healthcare.description: Public health insurance or comprehensive private plan; Czech healthcare is excellent

transportation.description: Monthly transit pass covers metro, trams, and buses citywide

entertainment.description: Charles Bridge walks, beer gardens, free concerts, galleries, parks

1M.lifestyle: You can live comfortably in Prague on this budget, though you will watch your spending. A one-bedroom in Vinohrady or Žižkov keeps you in walkable, interesting neighborhoods, and the excellent metro and tram system means you rarely need a taxi. Cooking at home most nights and eating at Czech pubs for the rest keeps food costs low. Winters are cold and gray, which is a real tradeoff.

housing.description: Spacious 2BR apartment in Vinohrady, Letná, or a modern flat in Karlín

dining.description: Mix of upscale restaurants, international cuisine, and traditional Czech dining

healthcare.description: Premium private insurance with top Prague hospitals; dental tourism is a bonus

transportation.description: Transit pass plus occasional car rental for countryside trips

entertainment.description: Opera at the National Theatre, jazz clubs, spa weekends, trips to Český Krumlov and Kutná Hora

2M.lifestyle: A spacious apartment in Vinohrady, Letná, or Karlín puts you in some of Prague's best neighborhoods. You can dine out several times a week at good restaurants, hold season tickets to the National Theatre, and take regular weekend trips around Europe on budget airlines. Prague's central location and low cost of living leave a comfortable buffer at this budget.

housing.description: Luxury 3BR apartment in Prague 1 Old Town or a renovated penthouse in Malá Strana

dining.description: Fine dining at La Degustation and Field, wine bars and tasting events

healthcare.description: International health insurance with coverage across Europe

transportation.description: Own car for countryside trips, transit pass, occasional train travel across Europe

entertainment.description: VIP concert tickets, private castle tours, premium spa retreats, European weekend getaways

3M.lifestyle: A large apartment in Prague 1 or Malá Strana puts you in the historic center with restaurants like La Degustation and Field as regular options. You can keep a car for countryside trips, travel around Europe frequently, and carry international health coverage. This budget goes far in Prague, leaving meaningful margin even after a comfortable lifestyle.

housing.description: Historic townhouse in Malá Strana or luxury penthouse with castle views

dining.description: Private chef for regular entertaining; exclusive wine cellar experiences

healthcare.description: VIP concierge medical with access to top European hospitals

transportation.description: Luxury vehicle, first-class European train travel, occasional private flights

entertainment.description: Private opera boxes, art collecting, exclusive cultural events, luxury European travel

domesticHelp.description: Full-time housekeeper, part-time cook, personal assistant

luxuryExtras.description: First-class European travel, Czech countryside estate weekends, art patronage, philanthropy

5M.lifestyle: A townhouse in Malá Strana or a penthouse with castle views, a housekeeper several times a week, and regular dining at Prague's best restaurants. You can fly business class around Europe, keep a nice car, and carry premium health insurance. Prague's low cost of living relative to the quality means a large margin even at this spending level.

housing.description: Grand historic palace apartment or villa estate in Prague 6 diplomatic quarter

dining.description: Personal chef team, exclusive dining events, private wine cellar and sommelier

healthcare.description: Elite European health plan, personal physician, annual executive screening

transportation.description: Multiple luxury vehicles, chauffeur, first-class European rail, private flights

entertainment.description: Art collecting, philharmonic patronage, exclusive castle events, luxury European grand tours

domesticHelp.description: Full household staff: chef, butler, housekeepers, driver, personal assistant, groundskeeper

luxuryExtras.description: Czech castle or chateau purchase, first-class global travel, art collection, philanthropic foundation

10M.lifestyle: A large villa or premium apartment in the Prague 6 diplomatic quarter, a cook who comes several times a week, regular housekeeping, and first-class travel throughout Europe. You dine at the best restaurants without checking prices and carry comprehensive private healthcare. Much of this budget will go unspent in Prague — the city simply does not have enough ways to spend $33K a month.
